hints.Agnus: 'Agnus' is a noun meaning 'lamb' — it is the subject
hints.Dei: 'Dei' is a noun in the genitive meaning 'of God'
hints.peccata: 'peccata' is a noun in the accusative plural meaning 'sins'

hints.to carry: 'to carry' is 'ferre' — 'tollis' comes from 'tollere' (to take away/lift)

hints.Imperfect: Imperfect of 'tollere' 2nd person would be 'tollebas' — 'tollis' has the present -is ending
hints.Perfect: Perfect of 'tollere' 2nd person would be 'sustulisti' — 'tollis' is present tense
hints.Future: Future of 'tollere' 2nd person would be 'tolles' — 'tollis' has the present -is ending

hints.pono, ponere, posui, positum: These are the principal parts of 'ponere' (to place/put)
hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the principal parts of 'ferre' (to carry/bear)
hints.duco, ducere, duxi, ductum: These are the principal parts of 'ducere' (to lead)

hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'agnus' would be 'agni' — 'Agnus' is the nominative form
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'agnus' would be 'agnum' — 'Agnus' is the nominative form
hints.Vocative: While this is a prayer addressing the Lamb, 'Agnus' here serves as the nominative form (vocative of 2nd decl. -us nouns is -e, but 'Agnus' is treated as nominative in this liturgical formula)

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'agni' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'agni' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'agni' ends in -i

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'Deus' is 'Deus' — 'Dei' has the -i genitive ending
hints.Dative: Dative of 'Deus' would be 'Deo' — 'Dei' ends in -i, the genitive singular
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'Deus' would be 'Deo' — 'Dei' ends in -i, the genitive singular

hints.Nominative: While 'peccata' could be nominative plural neuter, here it is the direct object of 'tollis'
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'peccatum' would be 'peccati' (sg.) or 'peccatorum' (pl.) — 'peccata' is nom./acc. plural
hints.Ablative: Ablative plural of 'peccatum' would be 'peccatis' — 'peccata' is the accusative plural neuter

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'peccati' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'peccati' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'peccati' ends in -i

translation: Lamb of God who takes away sins
